channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - gameinformer.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for gameinformer.com

item.title: New Metro 2039 Trailer Shows Off Impressive Visuals And Gameplay
item.content:encoded:
Metro 2039 is the fourth mainline game in the ongoing Metro series, and though we've been aware of its existence, today offered the first substantial public look at its gameplay. Along with showing off the visuals and environments, we also get to see gunplay and other mechanics, and learn about the Fuhrer Hunter, "the man who has washed the Metro in lies and propaganda to gain the ultimate power."

For more on Metro 2039, you can read our extensive preview on the game right here. Metro 2039 does not yet have an exact release date, but it is coming to Xbox Series X/S, PlayStation 5, and PC February of 2027.



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-games-showcase/2026/06/07/new-metro-2039-trailer-shows-off-impressive-visuals-and-gameplay
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Vivarium Is Looking Fresh In New Gameplay Reveal
item.content:encoded:
Amid a sea of zombies, shooters, and high-violence reveals at today’s Xbox Games Showcase, Vivarium was one of a small handful of games that offered a decidedly cozier aesthetic. Originally revealed back in 2023, this is the first time we've seen the game in action for some time.

Crafted by Studio Meadowflower and Serenity Forge, the game explores a small terrarium hidden away in the sun room of a small house somewhere in rural America. But within the glass house, a whole town of activity is unfolding. 

Players control 11-year-old girl, Jenny, as she navigates her days of endless summer in a world inspired by classic anime aesthetics like those seen in early Miyazaki films. Players talk to the electic (sometimes animal-like) townspeople, cook recipes, tend garden, collect stickers, decorate their home, and investigate a hidden secret to your tiny world. 

Vivarium looks beautiful and approachable. The game is targeting a 2027 release on Xbox Series X/S and PC. 



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-games-showcase/2026/06/07/vivarium-is-looking-fresh-in-new-gameplay-reveal
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Doom: The Dark Ages Is Getting An Expansion Called Revelations In July
item.content:encoded:
Revealed during the 2026 Xbox Game Showcase, Revelations is a new expansion for Doom: The Dark Ages that will be available on July 7. Id is using terms like "Hell freezes over" to describe the expansion and writes, "Wounded and betrayed, the Slayer is thrust into a merciless purgatory only escapable by confronting haunting truths and forging new strength with the help of a mysterious ally. He must ascend from the prison of his mind and fight an abomination of the gods to set his followers on the path to freedom."

The trailer for the expansion, expectedly, shows off violent and brutal first-person combat with a voiceover that says, "This place is your pain given shape." The Slayer is also using a new weapon, a glowing javelin, that appears to replace his shield.

Again, Doom: The Dark Ages' Revelations expansion will be available on July 7. You can read Game Informer's Doom: The Dark Ages review by following the link.



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-games-showcase/2026/06/07/doom-the-dark-ages-is-getting-an-expansion-called-revelations-in
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
